text:
Founder Lars A.
Engberg is based in Denmark and has extensive research history regarding urban renewal in Copenhagen and Aalborg, as well as the brownfield development of Hammarby Sjöstad in Stockholm, Sweden [cite: 12, 14, 16, 17].
**collaboration_potential**: High (**FLAG: Direct Collaborator**).
The project explicitly designs regenerative governance protocols for landscape-scale stewardship based on deep governance research [cite: 13].
**governance_model**: Ostrom-based regenerative governance protocol, bottom-up stewardship, governed by the Spiralweb Stewardship Association as a legal and institutional container [cite: 13, 15]
**open_source_tools**: Machine layer forming (JSON, schemas, evidence ledgers), Penguin Dashboard [cite: 13]
**community_size**: Stewardship tracks beginning at a micro-scale (10 m² base unit pixels) [cite: 13]

### What They Do
Planetary Guardians is a regenerative governance protocol designed for landscape-scale ecological stewardship, while SpiralWeb serves as its coordination infrastructure and knowledge commons [cite: 13].
The initiative connects place-based land stewards through a formalised protocol architecture [cite: 15].
SpiralWeb operates through "Field Nodes," grounding theoretical governance in highly specific, localized interventions [cite: 13, 15].
The system operates on an evidence-bound logic, where the base unit of stewardship is a 10-square-metre "pixel" of land.
Scaling occurs only when ecological indicators confirm stability and steward capacity is verified [cite: 13].
The architecture includes four layers: narrative orientation (cafés), field nodes, the protocol stack (methods and agreements), and a developing machine layer for evidence ledgers [cite: 13, 15].
The founder, Lars A.
Engberg, brings decades of experience in meta-governance, urban climate adaptation, and participatory design from Danish and Swedish contexts [cite: 12, 14, 16].
### Why They Matter for Planetary Governance Prototyping
SpiralWeb presents a highly rigorous, Ostrom-inspired protocol for connecting micro-level ecological actions (10 m² pixels) to macro-level planetary governance [cite: 13].
Their distinction between different support architectures (Land, Cultural, and Coordination) provides a readymade framework for a regenerative economy game mechanic [cite: 15].
The focus on "meta-governance"—steering relations and negotiations between actors without top-down coercion—is highly applicable to designing General Assembly simulators [cite: 12, 14].
Their use of JSON schemas and evidence ledgers bridges the Garden of Eden ecological focus with Spaceship Earth technological verifiability [cite: 13].
## ÆRTH

**Name:** ÆRTH
**Type:** Company / NGO (Science Cooperative)
**Orientation:** GARDEN
**Website:** https://www.aerth.live/
**Location:** Global (Switzerland-affiliated)
**Scale:** Early stage
**Maturity:** Platform in development

### Key People
Adina Popescu, Founder and CEO [cite: 18, 19, 20]
Brandon Levy, Head of Partnerships [cite: 20]
Sam Gbafa, Privacy & Interoperability Lead [cite: 20]
Janne Kuka, Product & Design Lead [cite: 20]
Ion Vonic, Chief Technology Officer [cite: 20]

### Partners and Collaborators
NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) [cite: 18, 21]
AROSE [cite: 18]
University of Western Australia (UWA) Oceans Institute [cite: 20]
Devonian Systems [cite: 22]

### Domain-Specific Fields
**funding_sources**: Gitcoin grants (GG23 OSS), bounties [cite: 21]
**key_projects**: Digital twin of Ningaloo Reef, Planetary AI, Global Environmental Science Data & Standards Cooperative [cite: 18, 20, 21]
**partner_orgs**: NASA JPL, AROSE, UWA Oceans Institute, Devonian Systems [cite: 18, 20, 21, 22]
**event_participation**: 2024 Climate Week, World Biodiversity Forum 2024, Horasis Global Summit 2025, Funding the Commons SF 2025 [cite: 18, 22, 23, 24]
**publications**: Academic abstracts presented at World Biodiversity Forum 2024 [cite: 23]
**collaboration_potential**: High (**FLAG: Direct Collaborator**). ÆRTH is actively building a dynamic digital twin of the planet to simulate ecological impacts, perfectly aligning with Spaceship Earth simulation goals [cite: 21, 25].
**governance_model**: Distributed environmental science cooperative, blockchain-secured data sharing with clear ownership tracking [cite: 21]
**open_source_tools**: Open source distributed environmental data set, blockchain computation standards [cite: 21]
**community_size**: Network of earth observation scientists on seven coasts [cite: 21]

### What They Do
ÆRTH is developing an AI-driven predictive platform designed to function as a dynamic digital twin of the Earth [cite: 25, 26].
Described as a "Kaggle for environmental science reimagined with blockchain technology," ÆRTH connects on-the-ground research streams with satellite observations [cite: 21].
The organisation unites Earth Observation scientists, marine biologists, and satellite integration experts to map and monitor ecosystem health, beginning with global oceans and reef systems like the Ningaloo Reef [cite: 20, 21].
The platform utilises blockchain to enable secure data sharing, allowing global researchers and machine learning scientists to compute over previously siloed data.
Governments and NGOs can offer bounties on the platform for scenario modelling and solution development [cite: 21].
Founder Adina Popescu views the project as a way to give the planet a unified "voice" based on real-time environmental data, leaning heavily into Gaia theory and planetary homeostasis [cite: 19, 26].
### Why They Matter for Planetary Governance Prototyping
ÆRTH is explicitly building a Planetary AI designed to assist humans in managing the planet by uncovering hidden connections within ecosystems [cite: 19, 25].
This is the ultimate "Spaceship Earth" dashboard.
Their platform allows for predictive modelling—such as simulating the disastrous ecological impacts of covering the Saharan Desert with solar panels—which is an essential mechanic for any planetary governance simulation or game [cite: 25, 26].
